blocks:
Ayahs

عَبَسَ وَتَوَلَّىٰٓ (۱)
He frowned and turned away (1)
أَن جَآءَهُ ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ (۲)
when the blind man approached him. (2)
وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّهُۥ يَزَّكَّىٰٓ (۳)
And how do you know, maybe he would purify himself, (3)
أَوْ يَذَّكَّرُ فَتَنفَعَهُ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰٓ (٤)
or take admonition, and the admonition would benefit him! (4)
أَمَّا مَنِ ٱسْتَغْنَىٰ (٥)
But as for someone who is wealthy, (5)
فَأَنتَ لَهُۥ تَصَدَّىٰ (٦)
you attend to him, (6)
وَمَا عَلَيْكَ أَلَّا يَزَّكَّىٰ (۷)
though you are not liable if he does not purify himself. (7)
وَأَمَّا مَن جَآءَكَ يَسْعَىٰ (۸)
But as for someone who comes hurrying to you, (8)
وَهُوَ يَخْشَىٰ (۹)
while he fears [Allah], (9)
فَأَنتَ عَنْهُ تَلَهَّىٰ (۱٠)
you are neglectful of him. (10)

Key Points

Based on the narrations of the Ahl al-Sunnah (Sunni sources), a person named Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum, who was blind, entered the assembly of the Prophet while the Prophet was meeting with the leaders of Quraysh, such as Abu Jahl and Utbah, inviting them to Islam. Since he was blind and could not see those present, he repeatedly asked the Prophet to recite the Quran so that he could memorize it. Consequently, a kind of displeasure and discomfort appeared on the Prophet's face. It was here that the initial verses of Surah Abasa were revealed, reprimanding and censuring the Prophet. However, in the verses under discussion, there is no clear indication that the person who frowned was the Prophet; the narrations of the Ahl al-Sunnah have raised this point. But in a narration received from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, these verses were revealed concerning a man from Banu Umayyah who was present with the Prophet and frowned and turned away when Ibn Maktum entered. (21)

The late Sayyid Murtada, in his book Mutashabih al-Quran, rejected the narrations of the Ahl al-Sunnah and, citing other verses of the Quran, states: Allah has praised the Prophet's character and said about Him: «انّك لعلى خلق عظیم» (22) (Indeed, you are upon a great character), and in two instances, He commanded the Prophet to be humble before the believers: «واخفض جناحك للمؤمنین» (23), «واخفض جناحك لمن اتّبعك من المؤمنین» (24). Furthermore, if the Prophet had such a character, based on other verses of the Quran, He would fundamentally not have succeeded in His propagation. As Allah addresses His Prophet, saying: «لو كنت فظّاً غلیظ القلب لانفضّوا من حولك» (25) (If you had been rude [in speech] and harsh in heart, they would have dispersed from around you).

Ten verses of criticism for frowning and scowling in the presence of a blind person—for whom smiling or scowling makes no difference—demonstrates Islam's sensitivity in dealing with the deprived class.

The meaning of «مَن إستغنى» (he who considers himself self-sufficient) is either financial self-sufficiency, which causes a person to consider himself superior to others, or self-sufficiency from guidance and instruction, which causes the person to view others as misguided and deviated. Of course, combining both is also possible, meaning a person becomes afflicted by arrogance due to great wealth and refuses to listen to the truth spoken by others.

21) Tafsir al-Mizan. 22) Al-Qalam, 4. 23) Al-Hijr, 88. 24) Ash-Shu'ara, 215. 25) Al-i-Imran, 159.

Messages

1- Physical disability is not a sign of defective character. (Respect for the blind, the disabled, and those with congenital defects is necessary.) «عبس و تولّى أن جائه الاعمى»

2- The value of morality lies in its intrinsic perfection, not in pleasing others. (The blind person does not see the one who frowns to be happy or upset.) «عبس و تولّى أن جائه الاعمى»

3- Islam opposes the spirit of arrogance and the humiliation of others. «ما یدریك لعلّه یزّكى»

4- One cannot judge based on the outward appearance of individuals. (Sometimes the blind person is more eager [for truth] than the sighted.) «الاعمى... لعلّه یزكّى »

5- The level of acceptance among people in society varies. In response to the Prophet's instruction and invitation, some groups achieve purification (tazkiyah), and some benefit merely from admonition (tadhakkur). «یزّكّى او یذكّر»

6- Giving admonition is not fruitless; it leaves its effect. «یذّكر فتنفعه الذكرى »

7- The fundamental factor is people's faith, not their wealth or capital. «امّا من استغنى امّا من... یخشى»

8- The Prophet is responsible for guiding people, not compelling them. «و ما علیك ألاّ یزكّى »

9- The feeling of self-sufficiency is condemned. The phrase «امّا من استغنى» is presented as a criticism.

10- The feeling of self-sufficiency and considering oneself complete and needless leads to deprivation from purification (tazkiyah). «من استغنى ... اَلاّ یزكّى »

11- The deprived class quickly turns toward Islam. «جاءك یسعى »

12- Inner reverence (khashyah) must be accompanied by outward effort and movement. «یسعى و هو یخشى »

13- The path to self-building is attending the presence of the Prophet or a divine master. «جاءك یسعى »

14- In the method of propagation, dual treatment of the poor and the rich is not permissible. «فانت له تصدّى - فانت عنه تلهّى »

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Abasa

This Surah has forty-two verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse and means "he frowned." Although the occasion of revelation for the initial verses of this Surah concerns an inappropriate interaction with a blind person, the central theme, like other Meccan Surahs, is the Resurrection (Ma'ad), the shocking events of the Day of Judgment, and the destiny of the believers and the disbelievers on that Day. Mentioning a portion of Allah's blessings upon humanity and man's ingratitude and thanklessness in the face of these blessings constitutes another part of the verses of this Surah.

Ayahs

كَلَّآ إِنَّهَا تَذْكِرَةٌ (۱۱)
No indeed! These [verses of the Quran ] are a reminder (11)
فَمَن شَآءَ ذَكَرَهُۥ (۱۲)
—so let anyone who wishes remember— (12)
فِى صُحُفٍ مُّكَرَّمَةٍ (۱۳)
in honoured scriptures, (13)
مَّرْفُوعَةٍ مُّطَهَّرَةٍۭ (۱٤)
exalted and purified, (14)
بِأَيْدِى سَفَرَةٍ (۱٥)
in the hands of envoys, (15)
كِرَامٍۭ بَرَرَةٍ (۱٦)
noble and pious. (16)

Key Points

«صُحف» is the plural of «صحیفة», meaning written (or scroll/page). «سفرة» is the plural of «سافر», meaning scribe (or writer), just as «اسفار» is the plural of «سِفر», meaning book. «بررة» is the plural of «بارّ», meaning righteous doer (or virtuous person).(26) The intent of «سفرة» is the envoys and messengers of revelation, meaning the angels who convey the Divine revelation to the prophets. These angels are the companions of Gabriel and are under his command, concerning whom the Quran states: «انّه لقول رسول كریم... مطاع ثمّ امین»(27) The path of Allah is both easy and possesses innate (fiṭrī), intellectual (fikrī), and jurisprudential (fiqhī) foundations.

26) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 27) Takwīr, 19 - 21.

Messages

1- The verses of the Quran are a source of remembrance (tadhakkur) and a factor in awakening human nature (fiṭrah). «انَّها تذكرة»

2- The status and rank of the Quran are beyond the reach of the unworthy and their attempts at distortion and alteration. «مرفوعة مطهّرة»

3- The Quran must be placed in a lofty position. «مرفوعة»

4- The Quran must be protected from all impurity. «مطهّرة»

5- There is no coercion or compulsion in the invitation of the Quran; whoever wills accepts it by his own choice. «فمن شاء ذكره»

6- That which Allah has honored, we must also honor. «صحف مكرّمة»

7- The Noble Quran is conveyed to the Prophet by noble angels. «صحف مكرّمة... بایدى سفرة كرام» (The source of the Quran is Allah, the Noble, and the Quran itself is noble, as are its conveyers, and the one upon whom it was revealed.)

Ayahs

قُتِلَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مَآ أَكْفَرَهُۥ (۱۷)
Perish man! How ungrateful is he! (17)
مِنْ أَىِّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقَهُۥ (۱۸)
From what did He create him? (18)
مِن نُّطْفَةٍ خَلَقَهُۥ فَقَدَّرَهُۥ (۱۹)
He created him from a drop of [seminal] fluid; then proportioned him. (19)
ثُمَّ ٱلسَّبِيلَ يَسَّرَهُۥ (۲٠)
Then He made the way easy for him; (20)
ثُمَّ أَمَاتَهُۥ فَأَقْبَرَهُۥ (۲۱)
then He made him die and buried him; (21)
ثُمَّ إِذَا شَآءَ أَنشَرَهُۥ (۲۲)
and then, when He wished, resurrected him. (22)
كَلَّا لَمَّا يَقْضِ مَآ أَمَرَهُۥ (۲۳)
No indeed! He has not yet carried out what He has commanded him. (23)

Key Points

Among the various ways the dead are dealt with, burying them in the grave is the method that Allah taught man. In Ayah 31 of Surah Al-Ma'idah, we read: «فبعث الله غراباً یبحث فى الارض لیریه كیف یوارى سوءة اخیه» When Qabil (Cain) was seeking a solution and thinking of hiding the lifeless body of his brother Habil (Abel), Allah taught him burial in the earth by sending a raven.

Messages

  1. The disbelieving and ungrateful human is cursed by Allah. «قتل الانسان ما اَكفره»
  2. In the method of propagation (Da'wah), sometimes one must speak sharply to a group. «قتل الانسان»
  3. Look at your own creation from a humble drop of semen so that you abandon disbelief and arrogance. «من نطفة خلقه»
  4. The creation of man is not accidental, but based on calculation, measure, and precise determination. «فقدّره»
  5. Creation is proof of power, and determination (measuring) is proof of wisdom. «خلقه فقدّره»
  6. Since the path to recognizing the truth and traversing it is easy, why do some groups go astray? «قتل الانسان ما اكفره... ثم السّبیل یَسّره»
  7. Allah has completed the argument (Hujjah) by making the path of perfection easy. «ثم السبیل یَسّره»
  8. Man possesses many potentials, which he achieves perfection in by traversing the path of truth. «ثم السّبیل یَسّره»
  9. Why does man, who witnesses death and the grave, remain obstinate? «قتل الانسان ما اكفره... ثم اماته فَاقبره»
  10. All affairs are truly attributed to Him. «اماته فاقبره»
  11. The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. «اقبره... انشره» meaning He brings forth the very thing He placed in the grave.
  12. Although the path is open and movement is easy, some groups remain stranded. «ثم السّبیل یسّره... لمّا یقض ما امره»
  13. Monotheism (Tawhid), Prophethood (Nubuwwah), and Resurrection (Ma'ad) are discussed alongside one another. «خلقه - السّبیل یسّره - انشره»
  14. The time of the occurrence of the Resurrection is solely in the hands of Allah. «اذا شاء انشره»
  15. Man, from beginning to end, is under His management and in His hands. «خلقه - اماته - انشره»

Ayahs

فَلْيَنظُرِ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ إِلَىٰ طَعَامِهِۦٓ (۲٤)
Let man consider his food: (24)
أَنَّا صَبَبْنَا ٱلْمَآءَ صَبًّا (۲٥)
We pour down plenteous water [from the sky], (25)
ثُمَّ شَقَقْنَا ٱلْأَرْضَ شَقًّا (۲٦)
then We split the earth making fissures in it (26)
فَأَنۢبَتْنَا فِيهَا حَبًّا (۲۷)
and make the grain grow in it, (27)
وَعِنَبًا وَقَضْبًا (۲۸)
as well as vines and vegetables, (28)
وَزَيْتُونًا وَنَخْلًا (۲۹)
olives and date palms, (29)
وَحَدَآئِقَ غُلْبًا (۳٠)
and densely-planted gardens, (30)
وَفَـٰكِهَةً وَأَبًّا (۳۱)
fruits and pastures, (31)
مَّتَـٰعًا لَّكُمْ وَلِأَنْعَـٰمِكُمْ (۳۲)
as a sustenance for you and your livestock. (32)

Key Points

Sabb (صبّ) means the pouring down of water from a height, which includes the descent of rain from the sky and the cascading of water from waterfalls. Qadb (قضب) refers to vegetables that are picked and eaten fresh, and it also includes grass and fodder for animals. The intent of shaqaqnā al-arḍ (شققنا الارض) is the splitting of the earth by plant sprouts, which cleave the soil to emerge from it and continue their growth. The intent of ḥabbā (حبّاً) is grains that are consumed as human food, such as wheat, barley, chickpeas, lentils, and the like. Ḥadā’iq (حدائق) is the plural of ḥadīqah (حدیقه), meaning a garden that has walls built around it, and ghulb (غُلب) is the plural of ghalbā’ (غَلباء), meaning a large, massive tree. Man must look at his food: Is it lawful (ḥalāl) or unlawful (ḥarām)? Do others have it or not? Where is the energy derived from it spent? And how was this food obtained? Imam Baqir عليه السلام, regarding the verse fa-l-yanẓuri l-insānu ilā ṭa‘āmihi (فلینظر الانسان الى طعامه), said: Man should look at whom he takes his knowledge from (28), thus considering ṭa‘ām (food) to include spiritual sustenance.

28) Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 50.

Messages

1- Man is commanded to reflect upon food as one of Allah’s blessings; eating without thought is the action of an animal. fa-l-yanẓuri l-insānu ilā ṭa‘āmihi.

2- If spiritual criteria are absent, man and animal are on the same level in enjoying food and nature. matā‘an lakum wa li-an‘āmikum.

3- Reflection on the origin of blessings is a means of eliminating disbelief (kufr). qutila l-insānu mā akfarah... fa-l-yanẓuri l-insānu ilā ṭa‘āmihi.

4- Existence is a school of theology (knowledge of Allah), both in the evolutionary path within man and outside of man. min nuṭfatin khalaqahu fa-qaddarah - amātahu - ansharah ṣababnā l-mā’a ṣabbā shaqqnā l-arḍa shaqqā...

5- The pouring down of rain and the growth of various plants and trees are purposeful, and providing food for humans and animals is one of the wisdoms behind it. innā ṣababnā l-mā’... shaqqnā l-arḍ... fa-anbatnā fīhā ḥabbā... matā‘an lakum wa li-an‘āmikum.

6- Grapes, olives, and dates are among the fruits recommended by the Quran. ‘inabā... zaytūnan wa nakhlā.

Ayahs

فَإِذَا جَآءَتِ ٱلصَّآخَّةُ (۳۳)
So when the deafening Cry comes— (33)
يَوْمَ يَفِرُّ ٱلْمَرْءُ مِنْ أَخِيهِ (۳٤)
the day when a man will evade his brother, (34)
وَأُمِّهِۦ وَأَبِيهِ (۳٥)
his mother and his father, (35)
وَصَـٰحِبَتِهِۦ وَبَنِيهِ (۳٦)
his spouse and his sons— (36)
لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِئٍ مِّنْهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ شَأْنٌ يُغْنِيهِ (۳۷)
each of them will have a task to keep him preoccupied on that day. (37)

Key Points

As-Sākhkhah (الصاخّة) means a terrifying sound that is close to deafening the ear. The intent of it is either the blast (cry) announcing the establishment of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) or the ear-splitting wailing and screaming of people on that Day. In the previous verses, the discussion was about livelihood (Ma'āsh); in these verses, the discussion is about the Return (Ma'ād). Regarding the motive for man's flight from his brother, mother, father, spouse, and children, several points can be made, including that he flees lest his brother demand his rights and thus entrap him. He flees so that he is not subjected to the demands of others. He flees so that others do not discover his disgrace. He flees so that he can attend to his own affairs and his fate may be determined sooner. «لكلّ امرى ء منهم یومئذ شأن یغنیه»

Messages

1- The Day of Resurrection is the Day of Flight. The flight of brother from brother, child from father and mother, man from spouse, father from son. «فاذا جاءت الصاخة یوم یفرّ...»

2- Kinship ties will be severed on the Day of Resurrection. «یفرّ المرء من اخیه و...»

3- On the Day of Resurrection, everyone is concerned with his own salvation. «لكلّ امرى ء... شأن یُغنیه»

4- On the Day of Resurrection, there is no opportunity to attend to the affairs of others. «لكل امرى ء منهم یومئذ شأن یغنیه»

Ayahs

وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُّسْفِرَةٌ (۳۸)
Some faces will be bright on that day, (38)
ضَاحِكَةٌ مُّسْتَبْشِرَةٌ (۳۹)
laughing and joyous, (39)
وَوُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ عَلَيْهَا غَبَرَةٌ (٤٠)
and some faces on that day will be covered with dust, (40)
تَرْهَقُهَا قَتَرَةٌ (٤۱)
overcast with gloom. (41)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْكَفَرَةُ ٱلْفَجَرَةُ (٤۲)
It is they who are the faithless, the vicious. (42)

Key Points

Mustabshirah (مستبشرة) means receiving news of a joyful matter from which the basharah (skin/face) brightens and opens up. Ghabarah (غَبرة) and ghubār (غُبار) mean dust settled upon something. Qatarah (قَترة) is black smoke that rises, and rahaq (رهق) means to encompass or cover. These verses divide people on the Day of Judgment into two groups: the people of felicity and the people of wretchedness, both of whom will be recognized by their appearance and faces. This is because the human face is the mirror of his inner character, and his internal joy and sorrow appear on his countenance. This Surah begins with a frowning face in this world and ends with a smoke-covered face on the Day of Judgment. عَبَس....تَرهقُها قتره Kafarah (كَفرة) is the plural of kāfir (disbeliever), and fajarah (فَجرة) is the plural of fājir (sinner/wicked person). The first refers to corruption of belief, and the second refers to corruption of action.

Messages

1- The Resurrection is physical (bodily). وجوه یومئذ...

2- A bright and smiling face is a virtue. مسفرة, ضاحكة

3- The laughter on the Day of Judgment is based on the glad tidings of a bright future. ضاحكة مستبشرة

4- Contamination with sin in this world causes the face to be contaminated on the Day of Judgment. ترهقها قَتره اولئك هم الكَفَرةُ الفجرة

5- Disbelief (Kufr) is the cause of committing sin, wickedness, and immorality. الكفرة الفجرة

6- The faces of the people of faith and piety are joyful and smiling. (By the implication that the faces of the people of disbelief and wickedness are smoke-covered.) وجوه یومئذ مسفرة... وجوه یومئذ علیها غبرة

7- Comparison is one of the best methods of propagation (Da'wah). وجوهٌ... وجوهٌ

8- Sin causes the pure, Divine countenance of man to be covered by an ugly, black veil. علیها غَبرة ترهقها قترة
